compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry is largely invalidated by the current visual state. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable remnant, though it is in a state of advanced decline.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_yellow_pot: Systemic Loss: Pot appears empty/soil only.
inventory_reconciliation.p2_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.p3_black_pot: Degraded: White rabbit anchor present, but plant material is severely desiccated.
inventory_reconciliation.p4_shared_black_pot: Systemic Loss: No visible silver guest foliage.

plant_audit.p1: Empty soil surface, no signs of life.
plant_audit.p3: Single leaf remnant remaining, showing chlorosis and severe turgor loss.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all visible pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshell fragments in P3/P4 area, likely an attempt at soil amendment or pest deterrent.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Presence of a paper receipt/document introduced in T-1, suggesting human activity near the biome.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t_minus_4: Initial observation shows a single green leaf in P3 and a rabbit anchor. P1 is empty.
temporal_deltas.t_minus_3_to_t_minus_1: Progressive wilting of the P3 leaf. The leaf angle shifts downward, indicating loss of structural integrity.
temporal_deltas.current: Total collapse of the P3 leaf. The biome appears abandoned or neglected.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The lack of chlorophyll-rich tissue, the downward postural collapse of the final leaf, and the absence of any secondary growth indicate a total failure of the indoor botanical system.

anomalies: Introduction of paper debris (receipt) in T-1.
Persistent use of eggshells as a substrate additive.
Complete disappearance of P1, P2, and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a catastrophic decline of the indoor garden. Over the five-day observation period, the specimens have transitioned from a state of marginal health to total systemic failure. The Pothos (P3) is the final indicator of this decline, having lost all turgor pressure. The environment shows signs of human intervention via the addition of eggshells and paper waste, but these have failed to support plant viability.
